VirtualAlloc: Allocates memory in the process. Often used by malware to prepare space for shellcode.
VirtualAllocEx: Allocates memory in another process's address space — a core step in process injection.
WriteProcessMemory: Writes data into another process's memory. Combined with VirtualAllocEx, enables code injection.
CreateRemoteThread: Creates a thread in another process — used to execute injected shellcode remotely.
NtCreateThreadEx: Native API for creating remote threads, used to bypass security hooks on CreateRemoteThread.
RtlCreateUserThread: Undocumented NT function for creating threads, used to evade API monitoring.
QueueUserAPC: Queues an asynchronous procedure call — an injection technique using APC hijacking.
NtQueueApcThread: Native APC queue function used for APC injection into suspended threads.
SetWindowsHookExW: Installs a system-wide hook to intercept messages — used for keylogging and injection.
SetWindowsHookExA: ANSI version of SetWindowsHookEx — installs global message hooks.
SetWindowsHookEx: Installs a system-wide hook to intercept events — keylogging and injection technique.
UnmapViewOfSection: Unmaps a section from a process — used in process hollowing to empty a legitimate process.
NtUnmapViewOfSection: Native version used in process hollowing to remove a process's original code.
NtMapViewOfSection: Maps a section into a target process — used for process hollowing and mapping injections.
IsDebuggerPresent: Checks if a debugger is attached — used to detect and evade analysis environments.
CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent: Checks for a remote debugger — anti-analysis technique.
NtQueryInformationProcess: Queries process internals — used to detect debuggers and analysis tools.
ZwSetInformationThread: Sets thread information class — used to hide threads from debuggers.
NtSetInformationThread: NT-level alias of ZwSetInformationThread — same anti-debug use.
Sleep: Pauses execution — used by malware to delay activity and evade sandbox time limits.
GetTickCount: Gets system uptime in ms — used to detect sandbox environments by checking elapsed time.
GetTickCount64: 64-bit version of GetTickCount — same sandbox evasion use case.
QueryPerformanceCounter: High-resolution timer — used to detect sandboxes via timing discrepancies.
RegSetValueEx: Writes a registry value — commonly used to add persistence via Run keys.
RegSetValueExA: ANSI version of RegSetValueEx — registry write for persistence.
RegSetValueExW: Wide version of RegSetValueEx — registry write for persistence.
RegCreateKeyEx: Creates or opens a registry key — often used to establish persistence.
RegOpenKey: Opens a registry key — may be probing for security software or configuration.
CreateService: Creates a Windows service — a common persistence mechanism for malware.
OpenSCManager: Opens the service control manager — prerequisite for service-based persistence.
StartService: Starts a Windows service — used to activate a malware-installed service.
MiniDumpWriteDump: Dumps process memory to a file — almost exclusively used to steal LSASS credentials.
GetAsyncKeyState: Polls the state of a key at any time — a primary keylogging technique.
GetKeyState: Gets the state of a virtual key — used for keylogging.
AdjustTokenPrivileges: Adjusts process token privileges — used for privilege escalation.
ImpersonateLoggedOnUser: Impersonates another user's token — used for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
DuplicateTokenEx: Duplicates an access token — enables token impersonation and privilege escalation.
OpenClipboard: Opens the clipboard — may be used to monitor or steal clipboard contents.
GetClipboardData: Reads clipboard contents — used by info-stealers to capture copied passwords or data.
InternetOpen: Initialises WinINet — the first step in making HTTP-based C2 connections.
InternetOpenUrl: Opens a URL directly — used for C2 communication or payload downloads.
HttpOpenRequest: Opens an HTTP request — used for structured C2 communication.
WinHttpOpen: Opens a WinHTTP session — alternative to WinINet, popular in backdoors.
WinHttpOpenRequest: WinHTTP equivalent — lower-level HTTP used by malware to evade WinINet monitoring.
WSAStartup: Initialises Winsock — required before any network socket operations.
socket: Creates a network socket — raw socket communication.
connect: Connects a socket to a remote address — indicates outbound C2 or exfiltration.
CryptEncrypt: Encrypts data using a key — used to hide payloads or encrypt stolen data.
CryptDecrypt: Decrypts data — used to unpack encrypted payloads at runtime.
CryptAcquireContext: Acquires a crypto provider — first step in file encryption, often seen in ransomware.
CryptGenKey: Generates a cryptographic key — used to encrypt files in ransomware attacks.
CryptImportKey: Imports an external key — used to load attacker-controlled encryption keys.
URLDownloadToFile: Downloads a file from a URL to disk — common dropper technique.
URLDownloadToFileA: ANSI version of URLDownloadToFile — downloads remote payloads.
URLDownloadToFileW: Wide version of URLDownloadToFile — downloads remote payloads.
ShellExecute: Executes a program or opens a file — used to launch dropped payloads.
ShellExecuteA: ANSI ShellExecute — launches programs or files.
ShellExecuteW: Wide ShellExecute — launches programs or files.
ShellExecuteEx: Extended ShellExecute — supports RunAs for UAC bypass.
WinExec: Executes a command string — legacy but still used by malware for simplicity.
CreateProcess: Creates a new process — used to launch payloads or spawn child processes.
CreateProcessAsUser: Creates a process under a different user token — privilege escalation technique.
GetSystemInfo: Retrieves hardware and OS info — used for fingerprinting the target system.
GetComputerName: Gets the computer's hostname — used to identify and profile the victim.
GetComputerNameEx: Extended hostname retrieval — used for detailed system profiling.
GetVersionEx: Gets the Windows version — used to select exploits or payloads for the OS version.
BitBlt: Copies screen pixels to a bitmap — used for screenshot capture.
StretchBlt: Copies and scales screen content — another screenshot capture method.
GetDC: Gets a device context for a window — first step in screen capture.
GetDesktopWindow: Gets the desktop window handle — used to capture the full screen.
PrintWindow: Captures a window's contents to a bitmap — used for targeted screenshots.
DeleteFile: Deletes a file — used by malware to remove itself or evidence after execution.
DeleteFileA: ANSI DeleteFile — removes files to cover tracks.
DeleteFileW: Wide DeleteFile — removes files to cover tracks.
MoveFile: Moves or renames a file — used to relocate dropped payloads.
CopyFile: Copies a file — used to spread or stage payloads.
ClearEventLog: Clears a Windows event log — used to remove evidence of malicious activity.
ClearEventLogA: ANSI version — clears event logs to destroy forensic evidence.
ClearEventLogW: Wide version — clears event logs to destroy forensic evidence.
FindFirstFile: Searches for files matching a pattern — used for file discovery and enumeration.
FindFirstFileA: ANSI file search — enumerates files on the system.
FindFirstFileW: Wide file search — enumerates files on the system.
FindNextFile: Continues a file search — iterates through files during enumeration.
GetFileAttributes: Gets file attributes — used to check for hidden or system files.
CreateToolhelp32Snapshot: Snapshots running processes and modules — used for process discovery.
Process32First: Gets the first process from a snapshot — iterates running processes.
Process32Next: Gets the next process in a snapshot — continues process enumeration.
EnumProcesses: Lists all running process IDs — used for process discovery.
NtQuerySystemInformation: Native API for system info — used to enumerate processes while bypassing hooks.
GetAdaptersInfo: Gets network adapter info — used to map the victim's network interfaces.
GetAdaptersAddresses: Gets adapter addresses — detailed network interface enumeration.
NetServerEnum: Enumerates servers on the network — used for lateral movement target discovery.
NetShareEnum: Lists network shares — used to find file shares for data theft or propagation.
TerminateProcess: Terminates a process — may target security software or competitor malware.
NtTerminateProcess: Native process termination — used to kill processes while bypassing API hooks.
OpenProcess: Opens a handle to another process — required for injection, dumping, or termination.
OpenProcessToken: Opens a process's access token — used for privilege escalation via token manipulation.
DeleteService: Deletes a Windows service — used to remove persistence or clean up after infection.
ChangeServiceConfig: Modifies a service configuration — used to alter persistence mechanisms.
ControlService: Sends a control code to a service — used to start, stop, or modify services.
OutputDebugString: Sends a string to the debugger — can be used as a timing oracle to detect debugging.
DebugBreak: Triggers a breakpoint exception — used in anti-debug code to crash under a debugger.
